response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior developer with broad full-stack and some systems-level background (C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux), but he does not meet several hard requirements: there is no indication of Japanese proficiency (native-level required), no Linux kernel investigation/modification experience, and no exposure to the core security and networking domains (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching, NETCONF/SNMP). His career trajectory centers on web platforms and product engineering rather than embedded firmware for network switches, making this a poor role fit despite strong general engineering skills.
strengths: Solid C/C++ experience building components for a professional C IDE (NI LabWindows/CVI)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) on Linux
Long track record of ownership across full development lifecycles, including testing discipline (TDD, CI/CD)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — a hard requirement for the role
No embedded networking or security-protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching, SNMP/NETCONF)
No demonstrated Linux kernel source investigation or modification experience

key_factors.factor: Strong general C/Linux and systems engineering background
